metadata.description: Challenges validating memory management including leak detection, GC pressure, large document handling, cache eviction, and coroutine scope cleanup

challenges.name: Memory Leak Detection (10K Documents)
challenges.description: Parse 10,000 small documents and verify that memory does not grow unboundedly. After parsing, clear all references and trigger GC. Memory should return to within 2x of the baseline. Tracks heap usage before, during, and after the parsing batch.

outputs.name: memory_baseline_bytes
outputs.description: Heap memory before parsing starts

outputs.name: memory_peak_bytes
outputs.description: Peak heap memory during parsing

outputs.name: memory_after_gc_bytes
outputs.description: Heap memory after GC and cache clearing

outputs.name: growth_factor
outputs.description: Ratio of post-GC memory to baseline

outputs.name: leak_detected
outputs.description: Whether a memory leak was detected

assertions.message: No memory leak should be detected after parsing 10K documents

challenges.name: GC Pressure Measurement
challenges.description: Measure garbage collection pressure during rapid document parsing. Parse 5,000 documents in quick succession and monitor allocation rate, GC frequency, and GC pause times. High GC pressure indicates excessive object allocation that should be optimized.

outputs.name: total_allocated_bytes
outputs.description: Total bytes allocated during the test

outputs.name: allocation_rate_mbps
outputs.description: Allocation rate in MB per second

outputs.name: gc_count
outputs.description: Number of GC cycles during the test

outputs.name: avg_gc_pause_ms
outputs.description: Average GC pause time in milliseconds

assertions.message: GC count must be a non-negative number

challenges.name: Large Document Handling (100MB+)
challenges.description: Verify that the system can handle very large documents (100MB+) without running out of memory or crashing. Parse a 100MB Markdown document and a 100MB CSV document, then generate HTML for both. Memory usage should remain bounded and the operations should complete successfully.

outputs.name: parse_completed
outputs.description: Whether parsing completed for each format

outputs.name: html_completed
outputs.description: Whether HTML generation completed for each format

outputs.name: peak_memory_mb
outputs.description: Peak memory usage in MB during the test

outputs.name: memory_within_budget
outputs.description: Whether peak memory stayed within the allowed budget

assertions.message: All format parsers must successfully parse the large document
HTML generation must complete for all formats
Memory usage must stay within the configured budget

challenges.name: Cache Eviction Under Memory Pressure
challenges.description: Verify that ParsedDocument HTML caches can be evicted to relieve memory pressure. Generate HTML caches for 1,000 documents, then call clearHtmlCache() on each and verify that hasHtmlCached() returns false. Measure the memory reclaimed by cache eviction.

outputs.name: caches_populated
outputs.description: Number of documents with populated HTML caches

outputs.name: caches_cleared
outputs.description: Number of documents with caches successfully cleared

outputs.name: memory_before_clear_bytes
outputs.description: Memory usage before clearing caches

outputs.name: memory_after_clear_bytes
outputs.description: Memory usage after clearing caches and GC

outputs.name: memory_reclaimed_bytes
outputs.description: Memory reclaimed by cache eviction

assertions.message: All 1000 document caches must be populated
All 1000 document caches must be cleared
Cache eviction must reclaim non-negative memory

challenges.name: CoroutineScope Cleanup Verification
challenges.description: Verify that coroutine scopes used for concurrent parsing are properly cleaned up after completion. Launch 500 coroutines for concurrent parsing, then verify that all coroutines complete and their scopes do not leak. No orphaned coroutines should remain active after the test.

outputs.name: coroutines_launched
outputs.description: Number of coroutines launched

outputs.name: coroutines_completed
outputs.description: Number of coroutines that completed

outputs.name: orphaned_coroutines
outputs.description: Number of orphaned coroutines after test

outputs.name: scope_cleaned_up
outputs.description: Whether the coroutine scope was properly cleaned up

assertions.message: No orphaned coroutines should remain after test
Coroutine scope must be properly cleaned up
